summaryText: Pastor Ryan Windauer opened the second week of the 'Christlike Character' series by addressing the topic of integrity. He began by welcoming the congregation and sharing a personal update about his growing family, creating a warm and relatable atmosphere. The core message centered on the story of Jesus cursing the fig tree from Mark 11, using it as a powerful metaphor for a life that appears fruitful on the outside (having leaves) but lacks genuine substance (fruit) on the inside. Pastor Ryan challenged the church to move beyond a 'leaves culture' of presentation and projection into a life of authentic integration, where one's private character matches their public persona. He clarified that integrity is not about perfection but about consistency and honesty, especially after making mistakes. The sermon outlined that integrity is seen by God, must be searched and tested by the Holy Spirit, and ultimately provides a fearless confidence and security. Pastor Ryan closed with a poignant story about a builder who cut corners on his final house, only to discover it was a gift for his own retirement, illustrating that we all must live in the 'house' our daily decisions build. He ended with an altar call, praying for those seeking to grow in integrity and for those desiring to begin a foundational relationship with Jesus.
mainPoints: Integrity is not perfection, but the integration of your beliefs and behavior, being the same person in public and in private.
True integrity is seen by God, searched by the Holy Spirit, and tested in the small, inconvenient moments of life.
Living with integrity provides security and fearless confidence, freeing you from the anxiety of a hidden life and positioning you for God's blessing.

versesMentioned.text: Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to find out if it had any fruit. When he reached it, he found nothing but leaves, because it was not the season for figs.
"Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence."
Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one.
Search me, God, and know my heart; test me and know my anxious thoughts. See if there is any offensive way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting.
One day Samson went to Gaza, where he saw a prostitute. He went in to spend the night with her.
Whoever walks in integrity walks securely, but whoever takes crooked paths will be found out.
The wicked flee though no one pursues, but the righteous are as bold as a lion.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 139:23-24 - "Search me, God, and know my heart..."
Monday.action: Begin your day with the prayer from Psalm 139. Ask God to reveal any area where your private life doesn't match your public faith.
Monday.prayer: God, search my heart today. Show me any 'offensive way' in me and lead me toward greater integrity.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 23:25 - "You clean the outside... but inside they are full..."
Tuesday.action: Identify one 'compartment' of your life (e.g., driving, online activity, private thoughts) where you tend to act differently. Consciously choose one action to align it with your faith.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, help me clean not just the outside but the inside of my 'cup.' Forgive my hypocrisy and integrate my life.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 10:9 - "Whoever walks in integrity walks securely..."
Wednesday.action: Look for one small, inconvenient opportunity to act with integrity today (e.g., correcting a mistake, telling the full truth, helping clean up a mess you didn't make).
Wednesday.prayer: Father, give me the courage to choose integrity in the small things, trusting that it builds a secure life with You.

Thursday.action: Evaluate a recent accomplishment or area of your life that 'looks good.' Ask the Holy Spirit if there is genuine, Christlike fruit (love, joy, peace) beneath the appearance.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, inspect my life. Where I only have leaves, prune me and cultivate real fruit that honors You.

Friday.scripture: Proverbs 28:1 - "...the righteous are as bold as a lion."
Friday.action: In a conversation today, practice letting your 'yes be yes and your no be no.' Speak and act from a place of integrated conviction, not people-pleasing.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, fill me with the bold, fearless confidence that comes from having nothing to hide. Let my words be true.

Saturday.scripture: Deuteronomy 6:4 - "The LORD our God, the LORD is one."
Saturday.action: Reflect on your week. Thank God for a moment you felt integrated. Repent for a moment you felt divided. Plan one way to carry your 'Sunday self' into your Saturday.
Saturday.prayer: God, You are one. Unify my heart and my life. Help me to be one person, wholly Yours, in every setting tomorrow.
